title: MoEs Are Stronger than You Think: Hyper-Parallel Inference Scaling with RoE
abstract: The paper introduces hyper-parallel scaling, a novel inference paradigm that allocates additional compute at test time to diversify a model's internal computations, thereby improving the quality of each token. It proposes RoE (Roster of Experts), a training-free inference technique that treats a single MoE as a dynamic ensemble by adding controlled stochasticity to the router's expert selection, running multiple stochastic forward passes per token, and aggregating the resulting logits.

methodology.approach: Training-free inference technique that adds controlled stochasticity to the router's expert selection, runs multiple stochastic forward passes per token, and aggregates the resulting logits
methodology.computational_optimization: Exploits overlap across forward passes, merges them into a single batched call, and introduces a specialized caching mechanism to reduce KV-cache size

key_contributions: Introduction of hyper-parallel scaling as a novel inference paradigm
Development of RoE (Roster of Experts) as a training-free inference technique
Optimization techniques for reducing redundant computation
